info.description: Proxy bridge for LLM API credentials.
info.title: Hivy API

post.description: Changes the authenticated user's password. Revokes all sessions.
Confirms a user's email address using a 6-digit code.
Sends a password reset link to the email address if an account exists.
Authenticates a user with email and password.
Revokes a refresh token.

get.description: Returns the current user and their organization memberships.

post.description: Sends a 6-digit one-time code to the given email address.
Verifies the 6-digit code and returns access/refresh tokens. Creates the user account if it doesn't exist.
Exchanges a refresh token for new access and refresh tokens.
Creates a user account with email and password.
Sends a new 6-digit email confirmation code. Rate limited to 1 per 60 seconds.
Resets a user's password using a reset token. Revokes all sessions.

Receives an HTTP request and dispatches it to the owning employee runtime for the specified trigger. The trigger UUID acts as a bearer token. If the trigger has a shared secret configured, the request must include the plaintext secret in any of: Authorization: Bearer <secret>, X-Api-Key, X-Webhook-Secret, or ?secret=<secret>.

parameters.description: Bearer <secret>. One of the accepted ways to send the trigger's shared secret.
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: Plaintext shared secret. One of the accepted auth header names.
parameters.name: X-Api-Key

parameters.description: Plaintext shared secret. One of the accepted auth header names.
parameters.name: X-Webhook-Secret

parameters.description: Plaintext shared secret as a query param. Last-resort transport when headers can't be customized.
parameters.name: secret

post.description: Receives webhook events directly from providers that require manual webhook URL configuration (e.g. Railway). The connection UUID in the URL identifies the org and connection.
post.summary: Receive incoming webhook from external provider

post.description: Exchanges a short-lived, single-use OAuth exchange token for an access/refresh token pair. The exchange token is obtained from the OAuth callback redirect.
post.summary: Exchange OAuth token for access and refresh tokens

get.description: Redirects the browser to GitHub's authorization page. Sets a state cookie for CSRF protection.
Handles the redirect from GitHub after authorization. Exchanges the code for a token, creates or links the user account, and redirects to the frontend with a short-lived exchange token.
Redirects the browser to Google's authorization page. Sets a state cookie for CSRF protection.
Handles the redirect from Google after authorization. Exchanges the code for a token, creates or links the user account, and redirects to the frontend with a short-lived exchange token.
Redirects the browser to X's authorization page. Sets state and PKCE verifier cookies.
Handles the redirect from X after authorization. Exchanges the code for a token using PKCE, creates or links the user account, and redirects to the frontend with a short-lived exchange token.
Returns API keys for the current organization with cursor pagination.

post.description: Creates a new API key for the current organization. The plaintext key is returned once.

delete.description: Soft-deletes an API key by setting its revoked_at timestamp.

get.description: Lists conversation assets owned by the caller's org. Optional filters: employee_id, conversation_id, path. Ordered by created_at desc, cursor-paginated.

parameters.description: Filter to assets uploaded inside conversations of this employee
parameters.name: employee_id

parameters.description: Filter to assets uploaded inside this conversation
parameters.name: conversation_id

parameters.description: Filter by exact folder label (empty = root)
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: Pagination cursor — created_at unix-nanos from the previous page's tail
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Returns audit log entries for the current organization with cursor pagination. Cursor is the last-seen entry ID.

parameters.description: Pagination cursor (entry ID) from previous response
parameters.name: cursor

post.description: Creates a checkout session for subscribing to a plan. The client chooses the provider.

get.description: Returns the org's active plan, provider, payment-method snapshot, and any pending plan change.

post.summary: Initialise a Paystack transaction for an upgrade quote

post.description: Resolves a Paystack transaction reference, asserts the paid amount matches the plan's price, and provisions the Subscription row.

get.description: Returns every integration provider in the catalog with action counts.
Returns a single integration with its full action list.
Returns all actions for a single integration, optionally filtered by access type.

parameters.description: Filter by access type (read or write)
parameters.name: access

get.description: Returns flattened schema property paths (up to 3 levels) for trigger refs and read action responses. Used for template autocomplete.
get.summary: Get schema paths for an integration

get.description: Returns all webhook event triggers for a single integration, including manual webhook configuration requirements if applicable.
Returns a conversation by ID.

delete.description: Permanently ends a conversation. Subsequent operations return 410.

post.description: Cancels the current in-flight LLM call or tool execution.

get.description: Returns pending tool approval requests for the conversation.

post.description: Approves or denies a pending tool execution request.

get.description: Returns webhook events persisted for the conversation, ordered by sequence_number DESC (newest first). Pagination cursor is the lowest sequence_number on the current page; pass it back to fetch the next older page. Filterable by event type.

parameters.description: Filter by event type (e.g. message_received, response_completed)
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Pagination cursor — sequence_number from previous page's tail. Returns events with sequence_number strictly less than this value.
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Returns persisted conversation events in chronological order, intended for hydrating a UI before opening the SSE stream. Paginated via since=<event_id>. Unlike GET /events, this endpoint sorts events ASC by sequence_number so the caller can render in order.

parameters.description: Return events with sequence_number greater than this event_id
parameters.name: since

get.description: Returns chat-ready messages aggregated from raw events. Consecutive same-name tool calls are grouped (e.g. "bash" called 11 times in a row → one group with 11 calls). Pagination is by sequence_number; aggregation is per-page so a tool call split across pages will not pair.

parameters.description: Max events scanned per page (default 200, max 1000). Aggregated message count may be smaller.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Sequence number from the previous page's tail. Returns events with sequence_number strictly greater than this value.
parameters.name: cursor

post.description: Sends a message to the agent in the conversation. Returns 202 immediately; response streams via SSE.

get.description: Opens a Server-Sent Events stream for real-time agent responses. Defaults to live-only (cursor "$"); clients that want history should hydrate via GET /v1/conversations/{convID}/history first. Resumes from Last-Event-ID when provided.
Returns credentials for the current organization with cursor-based pagination and usage stats.

post.description: Stores an encrypted LLM API credential for the current organization.

get.description: Returns a single credential by ID with usage stats.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a credential by setting its revoked_at timestamp.

get.description: Returns all custom preview domains for the current organization.

post.description: Register a new custom preview domain for the current organization. Returns DNS records to create.

delete.description: Remove a custom preview domain and its TLS configuration.

post.description: Checks that both DNS CNAME records are correctly configured and triggers wildcard TLS provisioning.

get.description: Returns Hivy dashboard metrics for the current organization.

Returns all employees in the org with enabled specialists,
skills (metadata only — no bundle content),
triggers, and the latest sandbox row.

Returns one employee in the org with enabled specialists,
skills (metadata only — no bundle content),
triggers, and the latest sandbox row.

post.description:
Queues a control-plane upgrade that snapshots the employee runtime SQLite database,
recreates the sandbox on the current employee image, restores the database,
syncs config, and verifies readiness. If an upgrade is already queued or
running for the employee, the active operation is returned.

get.description: Returns the current status and phase for a sandbox upgrade operation.
Returns employee runtime sessions with optional trigger delivery metadata.

post.description: Creates a new Hivy employee session.

get.summary: List skills attached to an employee

post.description: Creates an employee skill attachment.
post.summary: Attach a skill to an employee

delete.summary: Detach a skill from an employee

get.description: Returns all code-defined specialists and whether Hivy currently has each enabled.

post.description: Removes a specialist slug from Hivy's disabled specialist list.

delete.description: Adds a specialist slug to Hivy's disabled specialist list.

post.description:
Compiles the employee config, provisions an employee sandbox if
needed, pushes it to the runtime, and verifies readiness.
post.summary: Push compiled config to an employee sandbox

get.description: Returns generation records for the current organization with cursor pagination and filtering.
Returns a single generation record by ID.
Returns the authenticated user's non-revoked platform integration connections.

delete.description: Revokes a user's platform integration connection and removes it from Nango.

post.description: Creates a Nango connect session scoped to an existing connection, allowing OAuth re-authorization without creating a duplicate.
post.summary: Create a reconnect session for an existing connection

get.description: Fetches available resources of a specific type from the provider API. For example, list all repositories for a GitHub connection.
get.summary: List available resources for a connection

patch.description: Sets the webhook_configured flag to true on a connection, indicating the user has manually configured the webhook URL in the provider's dashboard.

get.description: Returns non-deleted platform integrations with safe fields for end users.

post.description: Creates a Nango connect session for the authenticated user to initiate OAuth.
Stores a connection after the OAuth flow completes via Nango.

get.description: Returns basic invite details by plaintext token. Returns 404 for invalid/expired/used/revoked tokens without distinguishing.

post.description: Accepts an invite and creates the corresponding org membership. The authenticated user's email must match the invite email.
Declines an invite and marks it as terminally revoked.

get.description: Returns models from providers that have at least one active system credential. Hidden routing-only models are excluded.

post.description: Creates a new organization and adds the requesting user as an admin member.

get.description: Returns the organization resolved from the request's auth context.

patch.description:
Updates workspace profile fields on the current organization.
Admins and owners only. Pass an empty string for optional fields to clear them.

get.description: Returns non-expired, non-accepted, non-revoked invites for the current org. Admin-only.

post.description: Creates a pending invitation for the given email and role. Admin-only.
post.summary: Invite a user to the current organization

delete.description: Marks an invite as revoked. Admin-only. Already-accepted invites cannot be revoked.

post.description: Generates a new token and re-sends the invite email. Admin-only.

get.description: Returns every user with an active membership in the current org. Any member may call this.
get.summary: List members of the current organization

get.description:
Returns the public catalog of billing plans, ordered by price
ascending (free first). Public — no authentication required.
Returns every provider in the catalog with a model count.
Returns a single provider with its full model list.
Returns the model catalog for a single provider.
Returns the platform integrations that can be used as RAG sources (i.e. their `supports_rag_source` flag is true). The admin UI uses this to filter the connection picker for the Knowledge Base.

post.description: Hybrid retrieval against the org's RAG dataset, optionally reranked.

get.description: Returns the org's RAG sources. Supports pagination and optional status / kind filters.

post.description: Creates a new RAG source that the scheduler will pick up on the next tick. Kind=integration requires a valid in_connection_id pointing at an integration whose supports_rag_source flag is true. Refresh / prune / perm-sync frequencies are validated against per-org minimums.

get.description: Returns one RAG source by ID with the last 5 index attempts inlined. 404 on cross-org access by design.

delete.description: Hard-deletes the source row. Postgres-side rows (attempts, documents, sync state, ACLs) cascade. Vector store entries are reaped later by the prune loop.

patch.description: Patches mutable fields (name, status pause/resume, refresh / prune / perm-sync frequencies, indexing_start floor). Only ACTIVE ↔ PAUSED transitions are client-settable; DELETING is server-managed.

get.description: Paginated, most-recent-first. Each row covers one ingest / perm-sync / prune attempt with status, doc counts, and error summary.
get.summary: List index attempts for a RAG source

get.description: Returns the attempt's status / counts / window plus the first page of per-doc failure rows. The dedicated errors page can be used to walk later pages if needed.
get.summary: Get an index-attempt with per-doc errors

post.description: Enqueues a one-off permission-sync job. Returns 422 if the source's connector does not implement PermSyncConnector (i.e. has no external ACL model worth syncing).
Enqueues a one-off prune job: the connector enumerates upstream IDs and the worker deletes any documents we have that no longer exist upstream.
Enqueues a one-off ingest job for the source. Set from_beginning=true to override the time-window floor and re-walk from the source's IndexingStart (or epoch).

get.description: Returns aggregated analytics from generations with flexible grouping and filtering.

parameters.description: Comma-separated grouping dimensions: model, provider, credential, user
parameters.name: group_by

parameters.description: Time granularity: hour or day (default: day)
parameters.name: date_part

get.description: Returns sandbox templates for the current organization.

post.description: Creates a new sandbox template with build commands.

get.description: Returns all public (platform-wide) sandbox templates that are ready.
Returns a single sandbox template by ID.

put.description: Updates a sandbox template. Resets build status if commands change.

delete.description: Deletes a template. Fails if agents still reference it.

post.description: Enqueues an async build job for the template. Poll GET endpoint for status and logs.
Deletes the existing snapshot (if any) and starts a new build. Can optionally update build commands.

get.description: Returns sandboxes for the current organization.
Returns sandbox details by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a sandbox from the provider and removes the DB record.

post.description: Runs an array of shell commands sequentially inside the sandbox. Stops on first failure.
Stops a running sandbox via the sandbox provider.

get.description: Lists skills visible to the current org. Use scope=public to browse the global library, scope=own for org skills, scope=all for both. Pass q to search by name/description.

parameters.description: Free-text search over name and description
parameters.name: q

post.description: Creates an inline-authored skill or registers a git-sourced skill for hydration.

get.description: Returns a skill with its latest hydrated bundle.

delete.description: Marks an org-owned skill as archived. Public skills cannot be deleted via this endpoint.

patch.description: Updates metadata on an org-owned skill. Public skills are read-only.

put.description: Replaces the current bundle for an org-owned skill.

post.description: Enqueues a fresh git pull at the tracked ref. Only valid for git-sourced skills.

get.description: Returns public Slack channels plus private channels where Hivy is already a member.

post.description: Invites Hivy to all public channels or selected channels. Joined private channels are treated as already available.
Executes a registered server-side LLM task using platform credentials. Each task name maps to a hard-coded definition (model tier, prompt, args). Caller may opt into streaming.

get.description: Returns tokens for the organization with cursor pagination. Supports filtering by credential_id.

post.description: Creates a short-lived JWT proxy token scoped to a credential.

delete.description: Revokes a proxy token by its JTI and propagates through cache tiers.

post.description: Returns a presigned URL the client can PUT to for uploading public assets (avatars, org logos, etc).

get.description: Returns aggregated usage statistics for the current organization.

schema_ref.description: references another schema by name for nested object resolution

configuration_notes.description:
ConfigurationNotes is markdown text shown to the user explaining how to
configure the webhook in the provider's settings.

webhook_url_required.description:
WebhookURLRequired indicates the user must manually configure a webhook
URL in the provider's dashboard for triggers to work.

last_event_id.description:
LastEventID is the event_id of the last event in this page. Clients
can pass it as the `Last-Event-ID` header when opening the SSE stream
to resume from exactly where history ended.

secret_set.description:
SecretSet indicates whether an HTTP trigger has a shared secret configured.
True when the trigger requires auth on incoming requests. The secret value
is never returned.

org_id.description: optional: scope token to a specific org

payment_channel.description: Payment-method snapshot from the most recent successful charge.

pending_plan_slug.description: Pending plan change scheduled at PendingChangeAt (downgrade flow).
